confirmed_issues.description: HeartbeatIntervalTicks used but never declared as an input variable in SMC_MarketDataEA.mq5
confirmed_issues.root_cause: Line 224 reads `g_heartbeatIntervalTicks = (HeartbeatIntervalTicks > 0) ? HeartbeatIntervalTicks : 6;`. The comment at line 44 says 'Initialised from the HeartbeatIntervalTicks input in OnInit()' but `input int HeartbeatIntervalTicks = 6;` is absent from the input declarations block (lines 23–28). The MQL5 compiler will reject this as an undeclared identifier. The check-mql-includes.mjs validator only verifies #include file existence and does not catch undeclared identifiers.
confirmed_issues.proposed_fix: Add `input int HeartbeatIntervalTicks = 6;` to the input block in SMC_MarketDataEA.mq5 after the TimerSec input declaration, matching the comment intent.

confirmed_issues.description: Prettier formatting violations in scripts/pipeline-watcher.js (2 locations) and scripts/reset-pipeline.js (1 location)
confirmed_issues.root_cause: String literals on single long lines violate prettier line-length and trailing-comma rules. Introduced in recent PR merges that did not run eslint --fix before push.
confirmed_issues.proposed_fix: Run npx eslint --fix on affected script files.

migration_blockers.description: EA will fail to compile if HeartbeatIntervalTicks is not declared — BUG-EA-001
migration_blockers.note: If the EA was compiled against a version where this was declared, it may still be running. But any recompile attempt on the current mq5 source will fail.
